content.problemStatement:
Chef is organising a contest with PPP problems (numbered 111 through PPP). Each problem has SSS subtasks (numbered 111 through SSS).

The difficulty of a problem can be calculated as follows:


Let's denote the score of the kkk-th subtask of this problem by SCkSC_kSCk‚Äč and the number of contestants who solved it by NSkNS_kNSk‚Äč.
Consider the subtasks sorted in the order of increasing score.
Calculate the number nnn of valid indices kkk such that NSk>NSk+1NS_k > NS_{k + 1}NSk‚Äč>NSk+1‚Äč.
For problem iii, the difficulty is a pair of integers (n,i)(n, i)(n,i).
You should sort the problems in the increasing order of difficulty levels. Since difficulty level is a pair, problem aaa is more difficult than problem bbb if the number nnn is greater for problem aaa than for problem bbb, or if a>ba \gt ba>b and nnn is the same for problems aaa and bbb.
constraints: in each problem, the scores of all subtasks are unique
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers PPP and SSS denoting the number of problems and the number of subtasks in each problem.
2P2P2P lines follow. For each valid iii, the 2i‚ąí12i-12i‚ąí1-th of these lines contains SSS space-separated integers SC1,SC2,‚Ä¶,SCSSC_1, SC_2, \dots, SC_SSC1‚Äč,SC2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,SCS‚Äč denoting the scores of the iii-th problem's subtasks, and the 2i2i2i-th of these lines contains SSS space-separated integers NS1,NS2,‚Ä¶,NSSNS_1, NS_2, \dots, NS_SNS1‚Äč,NS2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,NSS‚Äč denoting the number of contestants who solved the iii-th problem's subtasks.
content.outputFormat: Print PPP lines containing one integer each ‚ÄĒ the indices of the problems in the increasing order of difficulty.
